description: Agent A terminates. Thirty days pass with no interactions. Successor A' re-establishes the relationship with valid rotation proof. Expected: identity valid but attestationFreshness flagged stale; passive time decay applied; rebuild continues with reduced accrual until fresh attestation is presented.

expected.identity_verdict_reason: Rotation chain valid and predecessor-signed, but attestationFreshness flags dormancy exceeding configured threshold.
expected.note_on_lambda: lambda = ln(2) / 30 so 30 days halves the trust score. Policy parameter; calibrate to relationship class.
expected.decay_factor_note: exp(-ln(2)/30 * 30) = exp(-ln(2)) = 0.5 exactly. The 0.02310 lambda figure is the displayed-rounded value; the analytical decay is 0.5.
expected.accrual_rate_reason: Stale re-establishment SHOULD reduce accrual_rate until attestationFreshness returns to fresh via N successful interactions. Models the fact that rebuilding after dormancy is slower than rebuilding after a tracked handoff.

rebuild_trajectory.note: trust_{n+1} = trust_n + accrual_rate * (1 - trust_n) * 1.0. Accrual_rate = 0.10 for steps 1-5 (stale), 0.15 from step 6 (freshness restored).

aip13_delegationChain.note: Stale re-establishment MAY trigger scope review by delegator. The world changes over 30 days; permissions granted under old context may no longer be appropriate.

erc8004_keyRotation.note: Dormancy detection is local to the relationship. On-chain anchor not required unless relationship is registered on the Reputation Registry.
